title: taraxacum sublongisquameum
intro: Taraxacum sublongisquameum, also known as the long-scaled dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus. It is commonly found in mountainous regions and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals such as potassium and iron, providing various health benefits such as improving digestion, reducing blood pressure, and supporting the immune system. Taraxacum sublongisquameum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
